title: mfa — published events
description: Fourteen MFA-lifecycle events. Every dispatch is after-commit so downstream listeners never see uncommitted challenge state. Payloads are readonly value objects (readonly class + readonly properties) — immutable by construction so a listener cannot mutate the shape another listener depends on. Payloads carry `identity_id` (and optionally `challenge_id` / `credential_id`) only; downstream services that need email / dob / MFA state re-fetch through IdentityRepository so redaction rules apply consistently. No plaintext secrets, no plaintext recovery codes, no bytea public keys ever appear on an event payload.

events.name: MfaEnrollmentPreviewIssued
events.trigger: POST /api/v1/me/mfa/totp/enroll/preview returned a fresh TOTP secret to the caller. The secret is held ephemerally in Redis under a signed envelope; the caller must confirm (round 2) within 5 minutes.

payload.preview_id: string (ephemeral ID of the Redis envelope — used to correlate with the confirm event; NEVER the raw secret)

events.name: MfaEnrollmentConfirmed
events.trigger: POST /api/v1/me/mfa/totp/enroll succeeded — TOTP secret persisted to Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted via identity/identity's encryptor. Recovery codes generated + hashed atomically. Fires identity::MfaEnrolled downstream via the identity module's observer.

payload.elapsed_from_preview_ms: integer (duration from MfaEnrollmentPreviewIssued to confirm — signal for suspicious rushed enrolments)

events.name: MfaEnrollmentFailed
events.trigger: Round-2 confirm rejected — either the TOTP confirmation code was wrong OR the ephemeral secret expired OR the round-1 preview never happened. Ephemeral state discarded on failure.

events.name: MfaChallengeIssued
events.trigger: identity/auth::LoginAction issued a challenge because the resolved Identity has MFA enrolled. A row was written to the shared auth_mfa_challenges table with a 5-minute TTL + 3-attempt cap.

events.name: MfaChallengeSucceeded
events.trigger: POST /api/v1/me/mfa/challenge/{challenge_id}/verify succeeded. Challenge row consumed atomically; Sanctum PAT issued by identity/auth in the same transaction as the challenge consumption.

payload.credential_id: ULID | null (WebauthnCredential.id when method=webauthn; null otherwise)
payload.duration_ms: integer (issued_at → verified — signals user friction if too high, or automation if too low)

events.name: MfaChallengeFailed
events.trigger: Challenge verification rejected. attempts_remaining decremented; challenge row invalidates on zero. Fires MfaChallengeFailed EVERY time the code / assertion mismatch occurs, even mid-attempts.

payload.attempts_remaining: integer (0 = the challenge is now invalidated and the caller must restart from login)

events.name: MfaRecoveryCodeConsumed
events.trigger: A recovery code was successfully consumed in a challenge verification. The consumed entry was atomically removed from Identity.mfa_recovery_codes_hashed. The user is now down one code — LowRecoveryCodesRemaining evaluates in the same request.

events.name: MfaRecoveryCodesRegenerated
events.trigger: POST /api/v1/me/mfa/recovery-codes/regenerate succeeded. Old bcrypt-hashed array purged, new codes generated + hashed, plaintext returned ONCE in the response. Fires identity::RecoveryCodesRegenerated downstream via the identity module's observer.

payload.actor_id: ULID (Identity holder OR platform admin performing the regeneration on behalf of a user)

events.name: WebauthnCredentialRegistered
events.trigger: POST /api/v1/me/mfa/webauthn/register succeeded — attestation verified, credential row inserted into webauthn_credentials.

payload.transports: string[] (subset of usb / nfc / ble / internal / hybrid)

events.name: WebauthnCredentialDisabled
events.trigger: PATCH /api/v1/me/mfa/webauthn/credentials/{id} with `disabled_at=<now>` — user chose to temporarily disable without deleting. Preserves the row for the 90-day retention window (CleanupDisabledWebauthnCredentialsJob).

events.name: WebauthnCredentialDeleted
events.trigger: DELETE /api/v1/me/mfa/webauthn/credentials/{id} OR the 90-day retention window elapsed on a disabled credential (via CleanupDisabledWebauthnCredentialsJob).

events.name: MfaBackupCodeCountLow
events.trigger: MfaRecoveryCodeConsumed fired AND remaining_count < 3. Debounced 24h per Identity so a rapid burn-down doesn't spam the user.

events.name: MfaDisabled
events.trigger: All MFA factors removed from the Identity — TOTP disabled AND every active WebauthnCredential is disabled/deleted. This event is distinct from identity::MfaDisabled (fired by identity/identity when the TOTP secret column transitions to NULL); it fires when the CUMULATIVE MFA posture on the Identity is now zero factors — which trips a policy shift (any role-required-MFA becomes unenforced) so auth/policy needs to react.
